commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b8bf416bd2d3b85fe408edbeb0b13b24d3c6ac5b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a fetch-based `minimax` provider in `src/provider.ts`, unit coverage for dispatch/config/error handling, and a README provider entry.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR. Current main rejects `minimax`, while the PR diff adds provider dispatch and a concrete provider implementation.
commands.review_followup: - Human review should decide whether clawpatch core should own this MiniMax auth/provider surface; the only concrete patch gap I found is a small docs update.

target.title: feat(provider): add minimax provider for MiniMax Token Plan models
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `minimax` provider that calls the MiniMax OpenAI-compatible
chat completions endpoint directly via `fetch()`. This unblocks clawpatch
users with a MiniMax Token Plan when their default `codex` provider is
unavailable, rate-limited, or simply not their preference.

**Honest scope**: supports `map`, `review`, and `revalidate`. Does **not**
support `fix` — the chat-completions API has no tool or filesystem access,
so fix would record a no-op patch attempt. We refuse early with
`unsupported-provider` and a clear message that names the providers that
do support fix.

Closes #125.

## Behavior

- Provider name: `minimax` (dispatched alongside `codex`, `acpx`, `grok`, etc.)
- Default model: `MiniMax-M3` (selectable via `--model`)
- Default endpoint: `https://api.minimax.io/v1` (Token Plan base URL)
- Output is enforced via `response_format: {type: "json_schema", ...}`
  for the JSON-schema-validated operations (`map`, `review`, `revalidate`)
- `fix()` is intentionally unsupported: throws
  `ClawpatchError("minimax provider does not support clawpatch fix...",
  exitCode=2, code="unsupported-provider")`
- HTTP failure exit codes honor the provider contract (see
  `minimaxExitCode` below)

## Configuration

| Env var | Required | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MINIMAX_API_KEY` | yes | — | Token Plan API key (`sk-cp-…`) |
| `MINIMAX_BASE_URL` | no | `https://api.minimax.io/v1` | Endpoint override |
| `MINIMAX_MODEL` | no | `MiniMax-M3` | Default model |
| `CLAWPATCH_MINIMAX_TIMEOUT_MS` | no | `300000` | Per-call timeout |
| `CLAWPATCH_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` | no | `300000` | Fallback timeout |

## Usage

```bash
# Defaults (MiniMax-M3)
clawpatch review --provider minimax --feature feat_xxx

# Pick a different model
clawpatch review --provider minimax --model MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed --feature feat_xxx

# Override endpoint (e.g. for proxy or staging)
MINIMAX_BASE_URL=https://proxy.example.com/v1 clawpatch review --provider minimax --feature feat_xxx
```

`clawpatch fix --provider minimax` will fail fast with
`unsupported-provider` (exit code 2).

## Implementation

- New helpers in `src/provider.ts`:
  `minimaxTimeoutMs`, `minimaxBaseUrl`, `minimaxDefaultModel`,
  `minimaxFailureMessage`, `minimaxExitCode`, `runMinimaxJson`.
- New provider object `minimaxProvider` with the 5 standard methods.
  `fix()` throws `unsupported-provider` instead of calling the API.
- New dispatch case in `providerByName` between `grok` and `pi`.
- Exposed the 5 pure helpers via `__testing` for unit tests.
- README provider list updated to document the supported operation set
  and the explicit fix limitation.

## Tests

16 unit tests in `src/provider.test.ts` covering:

- `providerByName("minimax")` dispatch
- `minimaxTimeoutMs` env-var precedence
  (`CLAWPATCH_MINIMAX_TIMEOUT_MS` > `CLAWPATCH_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS` > default)
- `minimaxBaseUrl` default and override
- `minimaxDefaultModel` default and override
- `minimaxFailureMessage` classification for 401, 429, and generic statuses
- `minimaxExitCode` classification: 401/403 → 4, 429 → 5, other → 1
- `provider.fix()` rejects with `unsupported-provider` and exit code 2

`pnpm test src/provider.test.ts` reports **151/151 passing** (135
pre-existing + 16 new).

## Verification (final supported operation set)

**`check()` against the real `/v1/models` endpoint** (live, key in env):

```text
provider=minimax default-model=MiniMax-M3 base=https://api.minimax.io/v1
```

**`fix()` refuses cleanly** (no API call made):

```text
code     = unsupported-provider
exitCode = 2
message  = minimax provider does not support clawpatch fix: the chat-
           completions API cannot edit the worktree. Use --provider
           codex, acpx, claude, opencode, or pi for fix; use
           --provider minimax for map, review, and revalidate.
```

**HTTP failure exit code mapping** (via `minimaxExitCode`):

| Status | exitCode | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | 4 | auth failure (bad / missing `MINIMAX_API_KEY`) |
| 403 | 4 | auth failure (forbidden) |
| 429 | 5 | quota / rate-limit |
| 500 | 1 | generic provider failure |
| 502 | 1 | generic provider failure |

**Auth failure path with bogus key** (live, real API, 401 response):

```text
threw as expected:
  code     = provider-auth
  exitCode = 4   <-- auth, not generic
  message  = minimax provider auth failed (HTTP 401). Check MINIMAX_API_KEY.
             Body: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"authorized_error",
             "message":"login fail: Please carry the API secret key in
             the 'Authorization' f...
```

**End-to-end review** against `feat_library_d4f04bd776` (a real feature
in `ferminquant/budget`, same as the original PR description) completed
in 4m07s and produced 5 schema-valid findings with concrete
`evidence.path` + `startLine`/`endLine` references and
`suggestedRegressionTest` payloads. The full report is in
`/home/fermin/git/budget-pr-37/.clawpatch/reports/20260608T031651-3851f6.md`
on that worktree.

## Verification

```text
pnpm typecheck          # clean
pnpm lint               # clean
pnpm format:check       # clean
pnpm test src/provider.test.ts   # 151/151 passed
pnpm build              # clean
```

## Pre-existing test failures (unrelated)

The full `pnpm test` run shows 19 failures in `src/workflow.test.ts`
that exist on a clean `main` (verified by stashing this change and
re-running). They are unrelated to the provider change and not addressed
by this PR.

## Security considerations

- The provider never logs the API key or any response body in plaintext.
  Error previews use `safeProviderPreview(body, 200)` which limits
  exposure to a truncated body fragment.
- `response_format.json_schema` enforces structured output, reducing the
  risk of injection from model output.
- No new file-system or network surfaces are added beyond the chat
  completions endpoint.

## Addresses ClawSweeper review feedback

This revision addresses the bot's blockers from the first review:

- **P1 "Disable or correctly implement minimax.fix"**: fixed — `fix()`
  now throws `unsupported-provider` with a clear message and exit code 2.
- **P2 "Map MiniMax HTTP failures to auth, quota, and generic provider
  exit codes"**: fixed — new `minimaxExitCode` helper classifies 401/403,
  429, and others, and is used in both the run and check paths.
- **P1 "Add redacted real behavior proof for the final supported
  MiniMax operations"**: provided — see the **Verification (final
  supported operation set)** section above.
